claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 3 years old and does not live with you.
  - Blake is 2 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 3 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 3 (under 16)
  - Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 2 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 3 (under 16)
  - Someone who lives with the child is already claiming
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for UK Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin the Child Benefit eligibility assessment.

The first thing I need to know is about the child or children you're claiming for.

**How old is the child you want to claim Child Benefit for?**

- **Under 16 years old**
- **16 to 19 years old**
- **20 years old or over**

If you have multiple children, let's start with one and we can assess each child separately. Please tell me the child's name and age.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi, thanks for helping me with this. I actually have three children I'd like to ask about - Alex who's 3, Blake who's 2, and Charlie who's also 3. They're all under 16, so quite young still.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great, thank you for providing those details! With three young children - Alex (3), Blake (2), and Charlie (3) - this should be straightforward since they're all well under 16 years old.

Let me navigate through the assessment for children under 16.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Since Alex, Blake, and Charlie are all aged 2-3 years old, they clearly won't be working! So this is an easy "yes" - they're not in paid work.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
The next question is about benefits. Given their young ages (2 and 3), I can safely assume that Alex, Blake, and Charlie are **not** receiving benefits like Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right. Is that correct?

(This would only apply to older children who might be claiming benefits themselves.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right, none of them are getting any benefits themselves. They're too young for all that.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Exactly, they're far too young for that. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Again, given their ages (2 and 3 years old), Alex, Blake, and Charlie definitely aren't in any apprenticeships! So that's not an issue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now we need to check about **your** situation as the claimant.

**Do you meet UK residency requirements?**

You qualify if **any** of the following apply to you:

1. **You normally live in the UK**
2. You have **settled status** under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. You have **pre-settled status** and are working (earning above the National Insurance threshold)
4. You have **pre-settled status** and are actively seeking work
5. You have **pre-settled status** with sufficient resources to support yourself
6. You have **pre-settled status** and are studying with sufficient resources
7. You have **pre-settled status** as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national
8. You're a **Crown servant** living abroad

Do any of these apply to you? For most people, it's simply that they normally live in the UK.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK. I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, living in Manchester means you meet the UK residency requirements. Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check about your responsibility for the children.

**Are you responsible for Alex, Blake, and Charlie?**

You qualify if **any** of the following apply:

1. **You live with the children**
2. **You contribute to their upkeep** (paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit towards their care - this includes food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents, etc.)
3. **You pay maintenance** under a court order or agreement

Do any of these apply to you for all three children?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, it's a bit different for each of them actually. Blake lives at home with me. Alex doesn't live with me, but I help out with things like clothes and school dinners for them. Charlie doesn't live with me either - they live with their mum who already claims for them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying - that's really helpful information. Let me make sure I understand correctly:

- **Blake (2)** - lives with you ✓
- **Alex (3)** - doesn't live with you, but you contribute to their upkeep (clothes, school dinners, etc.)
- **Charlie (3)** - doesn't live with you and lives with their mum who already claims Child Benefit for them

This means we need to assess each child slightly differently. Let me continue through the assessment.

For **Blake** and **Alex**, you meet the responsibility requirement (Blake lives with you, and you contribute to Alex's upkeep).

For **Charlie**, since their mum already claims Child Benefit for them, this is an important factor we'll need to address.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
This is an important question. Let me check on the situation for each child:

**For Blake and Alex:** Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for them, or would there be any dispute about who should claim?

**For Charlie:** You mentioned their mum already claims Child Benefit for them. Only one person can receive Child Benefit for a child at a time. 

- Has Charlie's mum agreed to transfer the claim to you, or
- Would you both want to claim (which would be a dispute)?

If Charlie's mum wants to continue claiming for Charlie and there's no agreement for her to stop, then unfortunately you wouldn't be able to claim Child Benefit for Charlie while she has an active claim.

Could you clarify the situation for each child?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
For Blake and Alex, no one else is claiming for them - it's just me. 

For Charlie, no, their mum hasn't agreed to transfer it or anything like that. She's claiming it and Charlie lives with her, so I suppose she'll keep claiming it.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That makes the situation clear, thank you.

**For Blake and Alex** - no other claimant, so let's continue with the assessment for them.

**For Charlie** - since their mum is already claiming Child Benefit, Charlie lives with her, and there's no agreement to transfer the claim to you, you won't be able to claim Child Benefit for Charlie. Only one person can receive Child Benefit per child, and it's typically the person the child lives with.

Let me continue the assessment for Blake and Alex:

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check about where the children are living.

**For Blake and Alex**, are either of them in any of these special circumstances?

1. **In hospital** for an extended stay
2. **In local authority care**
3. **In a fostering arrangement**
4. Or are they in a **normal living situation** (i.e., none of the above)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: They're both in normal living situations - Blake lives at home with me like I said, and Alex lives elsewhere but not in hospital or care or anything like that.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, thank you for confirming. Both Blake and Alex are in normal living situations.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
We're almost there! One final question:

**Is the local council NOT paying towards Blake's or Alex's accommodation or maintenance?**

In other words, are Blake and Alex both being supported without any local council funding for their accommodation or upkeep? (This would disqualify eligibility if the council is paying.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right - the council isn't paying anything towards either of them. I'm supporting Blake myself, and for Alex I just help out with what I can.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! That completes the assessment. Let me now provide you with the final eligibility outcome for all three children.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Child Benefit Eligibility Results

Here's the outcome for each of your children:

### ✅ Blake (age 2) - **ELIGIBLE**
Blake lives with you, no one else is claiming, and you meet all the requirements. You can claim Child Benefit for Blake.

### ✅ Alex (age 3) - **ELIGIBLE**
Although Alex doesn't live with you, you contribute to their upkeep (clothes, school dinners, etc.), which qualifies you as being responsible for them. No one else is claiming, so you can claim Child Benefit for Alex.

### ❌ Charlie (age 3) - **NOT ELIGIBLE**
Charlie's mum already claims Child Benefit for Charlie, and only one person can receive it per child. Since Charlie lives with their mum and there's no agreement to transfer the claim, you cannot claim for Charlie.

---

## Next Steps for Blake and Alex

1. **Apply online** at [gov.uk/child-benefit](https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/how-to-claim) or by post using form CH2
2. **Provide documents:**
   - Proof of your identity
   - Birth certificates for Blake and Alex
3. **Be aware:** If you or your partner earns over £60,000 per year, you may be affected by the High Income Child Benefit Charge
4. **Payments** are usually made every 4 weeks (or weekly if you're a single parent or receiving certain benefits)

Is there anything else you'd like to know about claiming Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex (age 3) - ELIGIBLE: Alex is under 16, not working, not receiving benefits, not in an apprenticeship. You meet UK residency requirements (living in Manchester). You are responsible for Alex by contributing to their upkeep (clothes, school dinners). No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Alex is in a normal living situation and the local council is not paying towards their accommodation or maintenance. All eligibility criteria are met.

Blake (age 2) - ELIGIBLE: Blake is under 16, not working, not receiving benefits, not in an apprenticeship. You meet UK residency requirements (living in Manchester). You are responsible for Blake as they live with you. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake. Blake is in a normal living situation at home with you and the local council is not paying towards their accommodation or maintenance. All eligibility criteria are met.

Charlie (age 3) - NOT ELIGIBLE: Although Charlie meets the child-related criteria (under 16, not working, not receiving benefits, not in an apprenticeship), there is already an existing Child Benefit claim for Charlie. Charlie's mum is currently claiming Child Benefit for Charlie, Charlie lives with her, and there is no agreement to transfer the claim to you. Only one person can receive Child Benefit for a child at a time.
arguments.overall_reasoning: You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for two of your three children. Blake and Alex both meet all the eligibility requirements - they are under 16, you meet UK residency requirements, and you are responsible for them (Blake lives with you, and you contribute to Alex's upkeep). For Charlie, you cannot claim because their mum already has an active Child Benefit claim and there is no agreement to transfer it to you.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.question: Are you responsible for the child?
response.help_text: Responsibility means living with child OR contributing to their upkeep

criteria: You pay at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or equivalent in kind) towards their care
Contributions include: food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents
Can be weekly, monthly, or lump sum payments

paths.description: Paying maintenance under court order or agreement
criteria: Payments made towards child's maintenance under court order or agreement
Amount equals or exceeds Child Benefit payment

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to child location checks

response.description: Check if another person is claiming Child Benefit for this child
response.routing_logic: If no other claimant, proceed. If other claimant exists and parties agree who should claim, route accordingly. If dispute exists, defer to HMRC for arbitration.

response.description: Determine if special rules apply based on child's location
response.routing_logic: Check if child is in hospital, care, fostered, or living normally

response.question: Is the local council NOT paying towards the child's accommodation or maintenance?
response.help_text: You generally cannot get Child Benefit if the local council is paying for the child

response.description: Applicant meets all criteria for Child Benefit
response.reason: Meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements
next_steps: Apply online or by post using form CH2
Provide proof of identity for yourself and the child
Provide child's birth certificate or adoption certificate
Be aware of High Income Child Benefit Charge if you or partner earns over £60,000
Payments made every 4 weeks (or weekly if single parent or receiving certain benefits)

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex (age 3) - ELIGIBLE: Alex is under 16, not working, not receiving benefits, not in an apprenticeship. You meet UK residency requirements (living in Manchester). You are responsible for Alex by contributing to their upkeep (clothes, school dinners). No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Alex is in a normal living situation and the local council is not paying towards their accommodation or maintenance. All eligibility criteria are met.

Blake (age 2) - ELIGIBLE: Blake is under 16, not working, not receiving benefits, not in an apprenticeship. You meet UK residency requirements (living in Manchester). You are responsible for Blake as they live with you. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake. Blake is in a normal living situation at home with you and the local council is not paying towards their accommodation or maintenance. All eligibility criteria are met.

Charlie (age 3) - NOT ELIGIBLE: Although Charlie meets the child-related criteria (under 16, not working, not receiving benefits, not in an apprenticeship), there is already an existing Child Benefit claim for Charlie. Charlie's mum is currently claiming Child Benefit for Charlie, Charlie lives with her, and there is no agreement to transfer the claim to you. Only one person can receive Child Benefit for a child at a time.

response.overall_reasoning: You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for two of your three children. Blake and Alex both meet all the eligibility requirements - they are under 16, you meet UK residency requirements, and you are responsible for them (Blake lives with you, and you contribute to Alex's upkeep). For Charlie, you cannot claim because their mum already has an active Child Benefit claim and there is no agreement to transfer it to you.
